hoses: Supply line from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
FDC Supply Line A from Engine 181 discharge to sprinkler FDC inlet A
FDC Supply Line B from Engine 181 discharge to sprinkler FDC inlet B
sceneElements.elevation: Grade-level FDC connection. Third-floor sprinkler area used in one problem.

problems.title: Sprinkler System Support Pressure
problems.question: If the FDC is being used only to support the building sprinkler system, what pump discharge pressure should be set?
formulaBreakdown: For FirePumpSim sprinkler-only FDC support, use the standard sprinkler support pressure unless a building placard or department SOP gives a different value.
Standard sprinkler-only FDC pump pressure = 150 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: When Engine 181 is supplying only the building sprinkler system through the FDC, the FirePumpSim standard pump setting is 150 PSI unless a placard or local SOP states otherwise.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate this as an interior handline or standpipe attack operation. Sprinkler-system support uses the sprinkler support pressure.

problems.title: FDC Inlet Supply
problems.question: How many FDC inlets are being supplied during this sprinkler support operation?
formulaBreakdown: The operation shows two separate engine discharges supplying the FDC.
FDC inlet A is supplied by one hose.
FDC inlet B is supplied by one hose.
problems.instructorExplanation: Both FDC inlets are supplied to support the sprinkler system and reduce restriction through a single inlet.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count the hydrant supply line as an FDC inlet. The question asks how many FDC inlets are supplied from Engine 181.

problems.title: Third-Floor Sprinkler Support Pressure
problems.question: If the sprinkler system being supplied is known to serve the third floor, what pump discharge pressure should be set?
formulaBreakdown: Floor rule: 1st floor = 0 ft, 3rd floor = 20 ft.
Pump pressure = base sprinkler support pressure + elevation.
problems.instructorExplanation: The base FirePumpSim sprinkler support pressure is 150 PSI. Because the sprinkler area is on the third floor, add 10 PSI for elevation, giving a pump discharge pressure of 160 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not leave the pressure at 150 PSI when the supplied sprinkler area is known to be above grade. Add the elevation pressure for the third floor.
